hero.headline: I build frontend systems that scale without slowing you down.
hero.subtext: Product-first engineering. Measured impact. Clean execution.

metrics.text: Cut unnecessary re-renders by 35% by restructuring component boundaries
Improved onboarding completion by 18% by simplifying user flow
Built reusable UI systems adopted across multiple product surfaces

ctas.label: See How I Think About Product

saas.title: Scalable UI Is a System, Not a Page
saas.intro: This demo reflects how I structure frontend architecture around:
saas.introClosing: Without duplicating components or leaking business logic into UI layers.
saas.supportingLine:
The goal isn't flexibility.
The goal is controlled flexibility.

productThinking.sectionTitle: How I Think When Building Product

cards.title: Shipping Discipline
cards.headline: Speed is a strategy — not chaos.
cards.body:
Early-stage products need momentum.
I ship fast — but I design in a way that doesn't punish the team six months later.

Temporary decisions are documented.
Scalable decisions are deliberate.

cards.title: Reducing Friction
cards.headline: Every unnecessary step costs conversion.
cards.body:
Clear hierarchy.
Fast feedback.
Predictable interaction.

UI decisions directly affect revenue. I treat them that way.

cards.title: Feature Boundaries
cards.headline: Scaling breaks messy systems.
cards.body:
I separate domain logic from rendering logic early.
When teams grow, clean boundaries prevent expensive rewrites.

cards.title: Tradeoffs
cards.headline: Architecture serves the business.
cards.body:
I don't optimize for elegance.
I optimize for stage, team size, and risk exposure.

Sometimes the right solution is simple.
Sometimes it's structured.
Knowing when is the real skill.

tradeoff.intro:
Every system is a tradeoff.
The mistake is pretending it isn't.
tradeoff.towardSpeed: In early-stage products, learning velocity matters more than architectural purity. I bias toward iteration.
tradeoff.balanced: I design features with future scaling in mind while keeping implementation friction low.
tradeoff.towardMaintainability: In scaling teams, stability reduces cognitive load and protects delivery speed long term.

askMe: How would you structure a SaaS frontend from scratch?
How do you avoid feature-flag chaos?
How do you balance velocity and structure?
